retry_reason: Put operations unconditionally decrement refcounts; failure indicates a use-after-free bug, not a transient condition.
contract_summary: Release wrapper for afs_free_request: it calls key_put on rreq->netfs_priv and afs_put_wb_key on rreq->netfs_priv2 to decrement their reference counts. This function is the designated cleanup for the request; after calling it, the caller must not use the request or the referenced objects. The function unconditionally releases both references; there is no return value to indicate success/failure. It is intended to be called exactly once per request when the request is being freed.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to release two reference-counted objects (key and wb_key) stored in the request structure. No other logic is present.

check.reason: Both operations call put functions (key_put, afs_put_wb_key) on fields of the function parameter rreq. The access paths from rreq to the refcount fields (via .netfs_priv or .netfs_priv2 followed by .usage.refs) match the callee information. The type chains (starting with netfs_io_request and ending with atomic_t) are consistent. The segment counts between paths and type chains are equal.
